page: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 105 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier rating in cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth at 6,954.84 GB/sec, alongside strong uncached large-block read and write speeds. Passmark CPU and encryption tests both scored in the top tier, while database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrated strong performance. For AI workloads, the hardware-assisted Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions support strong prompt processing speeds across various LLM sizes, though text generation speeds for larger models remain average.

From a resource-density perspective, the r7i.24xlarge offers high memory capacity and memory bandwidth per vCPU, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. However, the lack of local storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which features a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps. This makes it less suitable for applications requiring ultra-low latency local disk I/O.

This server is designed for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, data analytics, and software compilation. It is also well-suited for enterprise applications requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios and cryptographic acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in cached memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU, and encryption tests, alongside strong results in database operations and Redis SET performance. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix operations. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and strong multi-core processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and CPU performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six vCPUs and DDR5 RAM for demanding database and in-memory computing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Top-tier cached memory bandwidth and encryption performance
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise apps
